name: Preparing Soups for Canning
recipeInstructions:
1. Select, wash, and prepare vegetables, meat, and seafood as described for the specific foods.
2. Cover meat with water and cook until tender.
3. Cool meat and remove bones.
4. Cook vegetables. For each cup of dried beans or peas, add 3 cups of water, bring to a boil for 2 minutes, turn off heat, soak for 1 hour, and then boil again for 5 minutes.
5. Drain the cooked vegetables and combine with meat broth, tomatoes, or water to cover.
6. Add seasoning if desired.
7. Fill jars halfway with solid mixture.
8. Add remaining liquid, leaving 1-inch headspace.
9. Adjust lids and process according to the recommendations in Table 1 or Table 2 based on the canning method used.
Table 1: Recommended processing time for soups in a dial-gauge pressure canner (at altitudes of 0-2,000 ft)
Style of Pack: Hot
Jar Size: Pints, Quarts
Process Time: 60 minutes for pints, 75 minutes for quarts
Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0-2,000 ft: 11 lb.
Table 2: Recommended processing time for soups in a weighted-gauge pressure canner (at altitudes of 0-1,000 ft)
Style of Pack: Hot
Jar Size: Pints, Quarts
Process Time: 60 minutes for pints, 75 minutes for quarts
Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0-1,000 ft: 10 lb.
